meta.description: Signal graveyard & pre-registration log. Every hypothesis MUST be registered here BEFORE testing. Prevents overfitting by tracking the denominator of tried signals.
rules: Register hypothesis BEFORE running any backtest or evaluation
Include specific metric and threshold for success
Update status after testing — never delete failed entries
Statuses: pending (not yet tested), accepted (passed threshold, deployed), rejected (failed threshold), graveyard (was deployed, later removed)

signals.hypothesis: Capping maxOdds at 2.0 improves ROI vs 2.5 or 3.0
signals.metric: backtest ROI delta vs no cap

signals.hypothesis: Filtering out league/market/direction combos with <50% historical hit rate improves ROI
signals.metric: backtest ROI for filtered vs unfiltered bets
signals.result: 1X2 bets filtered out (low hit rates). AH bets kept. 23/24 combos pass at 50% threshold. Only Serie A AH Home fails (49.1%).

signals.hypothesis: Requiring at least one team to be a regression candidate (xG divergence >= 3.0 goals over 10 matches) improves bet quality
signals.metric: backtest hit rate for variance-filtered vs unfiltered
signals.result: Core Ted filter. Regression candidates show higher CLV and hit rates in backtest.

signals.hypothesis: Filtering out matches where a team plays 3+ times in 8 days improves ROI (fatigued teams are unpredictable)
signals.metric: backtest ROI for congested vs non-congested matches
signals.result: Part of Ted's core filter set. Congested matches show degraded model accuracy.

signals.hypothesis: Adjusting match lambdas by 0.90x (crisis) or 0.95x (major) for injured teams improves prediction accuracy
signals.metric: log loss for injury-adjusted vs unadjusted predictions
signals.threshold: lower log loss on injury-affected matches
signals.result: Conservative multipliers deployed. Crisis (3+ key players out): 0.90x, Major (2+ key): 0.95x.

signals.hypothesis: Adjusting defensive regression expectation based on goalkeeper PSxG+/- improves AH hit rate (poor GK = less regression expected)
signals.metric: AH hit rate delta for GK-adjusted vs unadjusted regression

signals.hypothesis: Showing opponent SOS context on picks page helps human decision-making (qualitative — no backtest metric)
Using only post-manager-change xG data (when available) improves prediction accuracy for affected teams
signals.threshold: lower log loss vs full-season xG

signals.hypothesis: Using venue-specific xGD (home team's HOME xGD, away team's AWAY xGD) produces better CLV than blended full-season xGD. This is an architectural decision: Ted ALWAYS uses venue splits. If venue-specific doesn't beat blended, the model should drop the split and simplify.
signals.metric: CLV delta: venue-split model vs blended-xGD model on same bet universe
signals.notes: Not a filter — changes how lambdas are computed. A/B backtest: run solver with blended xGD inputs vs venue-split inputs, compare CLV on identical matches.

signals.hypothesis: Attacking overperformance regresses faster (5-10 matches) than defensive overperformance (15-20 matches). A model that uses different lookback windows per side — shorter for attack fade, longer for defense fade — produces higher CLV than symmetric windows.
signals.metric: CLV by regression type: attack-fade bets vs defense-fade bets at various lookback windows (6, 8, 10, 15, 20 matches)
signals.threshold: Optimal attack lookback < optimal defense lookback by 5+ matches; combined CLV > symmetric baseline by +0.5pp
signals.notes: Structural timing signal. If confirmed, changes when the variance-regression filter fires, not just whether it fires. Sweep lookback windows independently for goals-xG (attack) and xGA-conceded (defense) regression candidates.

signals.hypothesis: Using a rolling last-10 window for xGD inputs produces better CLV than an expanding full-season window. Recent form captures tactical shifts and personnel changes that full-season averages dilute.
signals.metric: CLV delta: rolling-10 xGD model vs expanding-window model
signals.notes: Ted frequently cites 'last 10' trends. Test windows: last 6, 8, 10, 15, full season. The solver currently uses full season — this would change data-prep inputs.

signals.hypothesis: CLV increases monotonically with model edge size. There exists a minimum edge threshold below which CLV is zero or negative (vig eats the edge). Mapping the full CLV-vs-edge curve reveals the optimal minimum edge and whether 'large edge = strong bet' holds. Collapses H07 (min edge), H09 (large edge), H10 (no edge) into one test.
signals.threshold: Monotonically increasing CLV across buckets; CLV turns positive at some identifiable threshold
signals.notes: Not a filter to deploy — it's a calibration test. Results inform what minEdge should be. If CLV is flat across edge buckets, the model's edge estimates are noise. If it's monotonic, model is well-calibrated and we can set minEdge optimally.

signals.hypothesis: When the closing line has moved 0.25+ goals toward the model's position since opening, remaining CLV is near zero. Bets placed after significant line movement toward you should be filtered out.
signals.metric: CLV of bets where line moved 0.25+ toward model vs bets where line was stable or moved against
signals.threshold: Post-move CLV < +1% (confirming value is gone); stable-line CLV > +2%
signals.notes: Requires opening + closing odds data (line-movement.ts signals already built but not registered). Ted's 2nd most referenced signal (151 statements). If confirmed, add as pre-bet filter: skip when sharp money already moved the line your way.

signals.hypothesis: Teams with BOTH attacking overperformance AND defensive underperformance (goals > xG AND goals_conceded > xGA) produce higher CLV when backed than teams with only one variance type. The intersection of H01+H04 is stronger than either component alone because defense regresses more reliably than attack, so net regression favors the team.
signals.metric: CLV of double-variance bets vs single-variance bets
signals.notes: Rare filter — few teams qualify simultaneously. May not reach significance (<50 bets over 3 seasons). If N is too small, mark as inconclusive rather than rejected. Ted described this pattern explicitly for Wrexham/Ipswich/Southampton/Leicester in Mar 2026.

signals.hypothesis: Blending MI model probs with 40% devigged closing market odds improves 1X2 ROI by filtering to higher-quality bets (Benter method — market signal enters twice: once in solver KL-divergence, once in output reweighting)
signals.metric: backtest ROI delta vs pure MI model (--ted --markets=sides --max-odds=2.5)
signals.result: +4.3% ROI (889 bets) vs +2.4% baseline (1456 bets). Hit rate dropped 52.6% → 50.5%. Profit +38.1u vs +34.9u. 85% market weight (Mack's rec) destroys edge — CLV collapses to +5.1% with only 3 bets. 40% is the sweet spot.
signals.notes: MI model already extracts market signal via KL-divergence minimization. Double-counting at 85% makes model = market (no edge). 40% preserves model signal while adding calibration. Market weight sweep: 10%→+2.1%, 20%→+1.5%, 30%→+2.2%, 40%→+4.3%, 50%→+0.8%. Next: bootstrap significance test.

signals.hypothesis: Blending MI model AH probs with 40% devigged closing AH odds improves AH ROI
signals.metric: backtest ROI delta for AH bets (--ted --markets=ah --max-odds=2.0)
signals.result: +2.7% ROI (452 bets) vs +3.1% baseline (725 bets). Worse across all edge thresholds. Hit rate dropped 54.2% → 51.8%. AH market already efficient enough that blending adds noise, not signal.

signals.hypothesis: Benter Boost at 40% market weight for 1X2-only bets produces statistically significant ROI improvement over baseline (not just noise from fewer bets)
signals.metric: Bootstrap p-value for ROI difference (Benter 40% vs baseline)
signals.notes: Run: npx tsx scripts/backtest-eval.ts --ted --markets=1x2 --max-odds=2.5 --benter --market-weight=0.40 --bootstrap. If significant, deploy Benter for 1X2 value bets only (not AH).
